summary: Large partial decompilation of the common fighter guard/shield module. The PR added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Guard.c` and its header, wired the new C object into `obj_files.mk`, and removed most of the corresponding assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`. The generated score report shows the new file at 91.76% overall, with many small guard, shield, physics, collision, and helper functions at 100%, while larger animation/IASA routines remained partial or guarded by inline assembly/MUST_MATCH fallbacks. Supporting changes refined fighter/common data fields, shield-related names, effect/library prototypes, vector/math helper macros, Yoshi guard interfaces, and decomp tooling output.
what_changed: Added `ftCo_Guard.c` with 3428 new lines implementing or partially implementing the common guard/shield code previously covered by `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Deleted 2792 lines from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`, leaving only a small amount of assembly and data for this region.

Added `ftCo_Guard.h` with public prototypes for guard helpers such as `ftCo_80091A2C`, `ft_80091A4C`, `ftCo_80091AD8`, `ftCo_GuardOn_Anim`, `ftCo_GuardOn_IASA`, `ftCo_GuardOn_Phys`, `ftCo_GuardOn_Coll`, `ft_80093240`, `ft_800932DC`, `ft_80093694`, and `ft_8009370C`.
Included `ftCo_Guard.c.o` in `obj_files.mk` so the new C translation unit participates in the build.

The PR report lists `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Guard.c` at 91.76% overall, with many functions at 100%, including `ftCo_80091A2C`, `ft_80091A4C`, `ftCo_80091AD8`, `ftCo_80091B90`, `ftCo_80091B9C`, `ft_80091D58`, `ft_80092450`, `ft_800925A4`, `ftCo_GuardOn_Anim`, `ftCo_GuardOff_IASA`, `ftCo_GuardSetOff_IASA`, and multiple simple Phys/Coll callbacks.

Some routines were still partial according to the score report, including `ftCo_800924C0`, `ftCo_80092158`, `ftCo_80093790`, `ftCo_GuardReflect_Anim`, `ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim`, `ftCo_80092908`, `ftCo_80092F2C`, and `ftCo_800921DC`.
Used `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` blocks with inline `asm` versions for difficult functions while also providing C versions under the alternate branch.

Refined `ftCommonData` in `src/melee/ft/types.h`, replacing several byte gaps with typed fields around shield/guard-relevant offsets such as `x260_startShieldHealth`, `x2A0`, `x2D4`, `x2D8`, and `x4C0`.

Renamed important fighter fields from generic names to semantic shield/player names, notably `xC_playerID` to `player_id`, `x1998_shieldHealth` to `shield_health`, and `x199C_shieldLightshieldAmt` to `lightshield_amount`.

Adjusted `ReflectDesc` and `AbsorbDesc` layout declarations in `common_structs.h`, including making `ReflectDesc.x20_behavior` a plain `u8` rather than an 8-bit bitfield and documenting `AbsorbDesc` offsets.
Added generic `SQ`, `MIN`, and `MAX` macros to `platform.h`; removed a local `MIN` macro from `dvd.c`; added `VEC2_SQ_MAG` and `VEC3_SQ_MAG` to `dolphin/mtx/vec.h`.
Added or corrected prototypes needed by guard code, including `efLib_8005F864`, `lb_8000D008`, `lb_8000D148`, `pl_8003E0E8`, and `pl_8003E114`.
Updated Yoshi-specific guard files and includes to integrate common guard logic with Yoshi shield behavior.

Improved tooling: `tools/report_score.py` switched asm-differ invocation from `-os` to `-mos`, captures stderr, and emits debugging data on JSON parse failures; `tools/asm-differ/diff.py` redirects make output/errors to stderr; `tools/vimrc` gained better offset grep commands.
smart_moves: Moved the guard module as a separate C object rather than continuing to grow `ft_08A4.s`, making progress measurable per function and easing later cleanup.

Kept difficult matching cases isolated with `MUST_MATCH` inline-assembly fallbacks while still writing readable C versions, preserving build/matching safety without blocking incremental decomp progress.

Added M2C stack helper structs in `ftCo_Guard.h` for functions using stack-local `AbsorbDesc`, `ReflectDesc`, and `Vec3` objects; this makes stack layout assumptions explicit for future matching attempts.
Promoted repeated math idioms to shared macros (`SQ`, `VEC2_SQ_MAG`, `VEC3_SQ_MAG`) instead of duplicating expressions in the guard code.
Renamed shield and player-id fields at the struct level, allowing guard code to read as `fp->shield_health`, `fp->lightshield_amount`, and `fp->player_id` instead of relying only on offsets.
Updated tool error handling around score reporting, which is useful for large partial-matching PRs where one failed asm-differ JSON output could otherwise obscure the whole report.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter action modules, partial matching can still be valuable when many simple Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks reach 100% and harder routines remain protected by `MUST_MATCH` inline assembly.

Before matching shield/guard behavior, invest in semantic `Fighter` and `ftCommonData` field names; many guard routines depend on offsets in the input, shield-health, light-shield, floor-normal, and timer regions.
Stack-local descriptor structs such as `AbsorbDesc` and `ReflectDesc` need exact sizes and offsets; documenting M2C stack layouts in headers can guide later decomp work.

Globalizing small macros like `MIN`, `MAX`, and squared-magnitude helpers can reduce noise and better mirror compiler output across multiple modules, but should be done carefully because they affect broad include surfaces.

Yoshi guard behavior is a special case in common guard transitions; common `ftCo_Guard` work should keep Yoshi-specific entry points available rather than assuming every fighter uses the same shield path.
Score reports are most useful when tooling captures stderr and preserves failed function context, especially for generated reports over dozens of partially matched functions.
naming_conventions: Common guard functions use the `ftCo_Guard*` prefix for action callbacks, e.g. `ftCo_GuardOn_Anim`, `ftCo_GuardOn_IASA`, `ftCo_GuardOn_Phys`, `ftCo_GuardOn_Coll`, `ftCo_GuardReflect_Anim`, and `ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim`.
Unknown but address-anchored common fighter functions remain named `ftCo_8009xxxx` or `ft_8009xxxx` when no semantic name is established.
Header include guards in newly touched headers follow a `GALE01_<address>` form, e.g. `GALE01_091A2C`, `GALE01_05B4B8`, and `GALE01_00CE50`.
Struct fields gained more semantic names when the PR had evidence: `player_id`, `shield_health`, and `lightshield_amount` replaced more generic offset-derived names.
M2C stack helper structs are named `_m2c_stack_<function>`, for example `_m2c_stack_ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim` and `_m2c_stack_ftCo_80092F2C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used mixed C/inline-asm functions controlled by `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` to preserve exact matching paths while keeping a C implementation for readability and future cleanup.
Inline assembly blocks use `nofralloc`, original instruction comments, and local labels renamed from `.L_...` to `lbl_...` inside C asm functions.

Defined file-local float/double constants under `MWERKS_GEKKO`, including `ftCo_804D8538`, `ftCo_804D853C`, `ftCo_804D8540`, `ftCo_804D8568`, and related constants, to reproduce the rodata layout used by the original guard assembly.
The score report documents a pragmatic target: accept 91.76% file score with many 100% functions, leaving known hard functions for later matching rather than blocking the whole module.
Used fake/explicit artifacts where necessary, such as documented `@todo Unused stack`, `@todo Inline depth`, and a `!gobj;` fake in one C branch, rather than hiding unresolved matching constraints.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match the partial `ftCo_Guard.c` routines with low scores in PR #861, especially `ftCo_800924C0`, `ftCo_80092158`, `ftCo_GuardReflect_Anim`, and `ftCo_GuardSetOff_Anim`.
Search for subsequent renames of `ftCo_80091A2C`, `ft_80091A4C`, `ftCo_80091AD8`, and other address-named guard helpers.
Look for later changes to `ftCommonData` offsets around `x260` through `x2F4` and `x4B0` through `x4C0` to see which guard constants gained semantic names.
Search Yoshi guard PRs touching `ftYs_Guard` and common guard transitions to verify how Yoshi shield special cases were eventually documented.
Find whether the `MUST_MATCH` inline asm blocks in `ftCo_Guard.c` were removed or replaced by matched C in later work.
